title: Rally to SHOW ME Abolition in Missouri on March 10
author: Kevan Myers
orientation: A call-to-action announcement for a March 10, 2021 abolitionist rally at the Missouri State Capitol in Jefferson City supporting State Senator Mike Moon's Abolition of Abortion in Missouri Act. Frames the rally as the fifth annual gathering and contrasts the abolitionist demand for equal protection of preborn children against pro-life regulation of abortion.
audiences: Pro-life advocate weighing the abolitionist critique
State legislator or staffer evaluating the bill
Pastor discerning a biblical response to abortion law

questions.question: When and where is the rally happening, and what will participants do there?
questions.answer: Abolitionists will gather at 2 p.m. on Wednesday, March 10th in the rotunda of the Missouri State Capitol in Jefferson City to worship, hear preaching, and demand the abolition of preborn murder.
questions.quote: Abolitionists are heading to Jefferson City on Wednesday March 10th to rally for the Abolition of Abortion in Missouri Act. We will be gathering in the rotunda of the Capitol building at 2 p.m. to sing praises to our God, hear preaching from the Word of God, and demand the abolition of preborn murder in the Show Me State.

questions.question: Who sponsored the legislation and what does it actually do?
questions.audience: State legislator or staffer evaluating the bill
questions.answer: State Senator Mike Moon authored the Abolition of Abortion in Missouri Act, which is written to establish equal protection from murder for the preborn, and the rally aims to enjoin other legislators to stand with him.
questions.quote: State Senator Mike Moon has put forward this excellent piece of legislation which is written to establish equal protection from murder for the preborn.  We are rallying to support him and enjoin legislators to stand with him and thereby fulfill their obligation to protect those under their jurisdiction.

questions.question: Why does the author argue that Missouri's existing pro-life laws are inadequate rather than worth celebrating?
questions.audience: Pro-life advocate weighing the abolitionist critique
questions.answer: Myers argues that pro-life regulatory bills merely govern the particulars of how children are killed in the womb instead of treating abortion as the murder it is, training Christians to think pro-life rather than biblically.
questions.quote: These bills are written and passed to regulate the “what, who, when, where, and how” particulars of murdering children in the womb in the Show Me State.

questions.question: How does the article distinguish abolitionist conviction from a pro-life posture?
questions.audience: Pastor discerning a biblical response to abortion law
questions.answer: The author claims abolitionists were struck with the truth that regulatory bills do not tell the truth about abortion as MURDER and instead treat it as if it were healthcare, marking a sharp departure from the pro-life framing.
questions.quote: Five years ago, the spark of the abolitionism caught fire in Missouri and Christians began to be struck with the truth that bills which regulate abortion do NOT tell the truth about abortion. The do not treat abortion like what it is: **MURDER**. Instead, those bills treat abortion as if it were healthcare.

questions.question: What specific examples of pro-life compromises does the article reject?
questions.audience: Pro-life advocate weighing the abolitionist critique
questions.answer: The article lists wider hallways, informed consent, waiting periods, licensed-professional rules, 20-week bans, heartbeat bills, and abortion exceptions as low compromises that regulate murder under color of law rather than abolishing it.
questions.quote: We have spent much time and money in making rules regarding wider hallways, informed consent, waiting periods, licensed professionals, 20-week bans, heartbeat bills, and evil abortion exceptions. We have stooped low in regulating *who* can murder, *when* they can murder, *where* they can murder, and *how* they can murder under color of “law,” rather than actually demanding the abolition of that murder under law.

questions.question: Which Scriptures does the author marshal to indict the pro-life establishment?
questions.audience: Pastor discerning a biblical response to abortion law
questions.answer: Myers cites Jeremiah 22:3 against shedding innocent blood, Isaiah 1:6 on the rotten state of society, Jeremiah 8:11 to accuse pro-lifers of crying peace where there is no peace, and Isaiah 1:16-17 to call for ceasing evil and learning to do well.
questions.quote: In such a day, in our society, when there is *“no soundness in it, but wounds, bruises, and putrifying sores”* (Isaiah 1:6), the Pro-Life Establishment has *“healed the hurt of the daughter of my people slightly, saying, Peace, peace; when there is no peace”* (Jeremiah 8:11).  We don’t need bandaids on gangrene.  We need the cure.

questions.question: How long has the abolitionist movement been pushing this bill, and what has happened to it?
questions.answer: This is the fifth annual rally and the fourth year a bill to abolish abortion has been introduced in Missouri, and pro-life leaders in the state have so far prevented the bill from even receiving a committee hearing.
questions.quote: This is now the fifth year that abolitionists have held a rally at the Missouri State Capitol, and the fourth year that a bill to abolish abortion has been put forth in our state. Each year, the pro-life leaders in the state of Missouri have kept the bill from even receiving a committee hearing.

questions.question: What does the author ask of readers who can't attend the rally in person?
questions.answer: Non-Missourians are asked to share the article with someone who lives in the state so the collective cry for justice keeps growing.
questions.quote: If you live in Missouri, I hope you will join us. If you don’t live in Missouri, share this article with someone who does.

related.title: Missouri is not Abortion-Free. But Thanks to Sen. Mike Moon, it Can Be.
related.reason: Companion piece from the prior month explaining the same Mike Moon legislation that this rally supports.

related.title: Hearing for Abolition of Abortion in Missouri Act Scheduled For Wednesday 3/10
related.reason: Direct follow-up: a committee hearing was scheduled for the same day as the rally, breaking the pattern lamented in this article.

related.title: Missouri Abolition Bill Author Mike Moon Elected to State Senate
related.reason: Background on how the bill's author landed in the seat that lets him introduce the legislation referenced here.

related.title: Point of Order! How Missouri Baptists Killed the Resolution on Abolishing Abortion.
related.reason: Later Missouri-specific account of institutional obstruction, echoing the article's complaint that pro-life leaders block abolition efforts.

related.title: Biblical, Not Secular
related.reason: The article explicitly hyperlinks this resource as the standard for being biblical rather than merely pro-life on abortion.

related.title: Abolitionist, Not Pro-Life
related.reason: Develops the pro-life vs. abolitionist distinction that drives this rally announcement.

related.title: Immediate, Not Gradual Abolition of Abortion
related.reason: Argues against the regulatory, incremental strategy this article condemns as stooping low to regulate murder.
